$comment: Reviewed npm-audit allowlist (P1 W6-T4, ADR-0016 D16 pipeline).
npm audit has no native ignore file, so CI runs `npm audit --json` and pipes
it through scripts/npm-audit-gate.mjs, which FAILS on any advisory at or above
the configured severity floor (high) whose GHSA id is NOT listed below.
This mirrors the reviewed .trivyignore convention: never a blanket ignore —
every entry has a justification + an expiry (re-review) date. An entry past
its expiry makes the gate fail until it is re-reviewed and renewed or removed.

allow.advisory: React Router XSS via open redirects
allow.justification: Fix requires react-router-dom>=6.30.4 which npm reports is OUTSIDE the pinned 6.30.0 dependency range; the dep-version bump is out of scope for W6-T4 (no while-here bumps) and is tracked for the W6 frontend dep-bump pass. The platform does not currently consume untrusted redirect targets in router config; reachability is low. Accepted pending the bump.

allow.advisory: form-data CRLF injection via unescaped multipart field names
allow.justification: Present only in the dev/test dependency tree (not shipped in the vite production bundle). Fix is a transitive bump pending upstream test-tooling release. Not part of the deployed artifact; no runtime exposure.
